title: Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge
summary: A controlled log for comparing sightings by time, place, direction, witness reliability, and distinguishing details before passing a consolidated report to authorities.
content:
A controlled log for comparing sightings by time, place, direction, witness reliability, and distinguishing details before passing a consolidated report to authorities.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Give every incoming sighting a unique number and record the witness’s direct observations separately from conclusions or rumours.
Compare exact time, position, direction, clothing, companions, vehicle, speech, gait, and other distinguishing details against the verified description.
Plot plausible sightings in time order, mark contradictions and duplicates, and contact the witness again only through the designated coordinator.
Send authorities a concise consolidated update that preserves original forms and clearly labels confirmed, plausible, conflicting, and disproved reports.
warnings: Do not redirect an official search from an unverified social-media claim.
Do not reveal the missing person’s private details beyond operational need.
common_mistakes: Starting Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Missing-person sighting verification and duplicate-report merge.']
